extractions.problem: The cellular expression pattern of the endolysosomal cation channel TRPML3 on a whole-tissue level remains largely unknown, unlike the ubiquitously expressed TRPML1.
extractions.method: Generated a τGFP reporter mouse model (Trpml3 IRES-Cre/eR26-τGFP) and compared expression data via immunofluorescence, immunohistochemistry with Trpml3-/- knockout controls, in situ hybridization, and RT-qPCR across multiple organs.
extractions.finding: TRPML3 is expressed in alveolar macrophages, melanocytes, kidney collecting duct principal cells, olfactory sensory neurons, and multiple endocrine/secretory glands (parathyroid, thyroid, salivary, adrenal, pituitary, testes, ovaries), suggesting roles in hormone secretion or uptake.
extractions.dataset: Trpml3 IRES-Cre/eR26-τGFP reporter mice, CD1 mice, Trpml3-/- knockout littermates (~75% C57BL/6, ~25% Sv129/Ola)
extractions.metric: RT-qPCR relative expression normalized to 18S rRNA
extractions.baseline: Trpml3-/- knockout mice (negative control for immunoreactivity specificity)
extractions.result: Trpml3 mRNA detected in RPE, thymus, back skin, kidney, lung; not detected in cerebellum, heart, muscle, stomach, small intestine, colon

extractions.problem: Understanding the autoimmune progression in Type 1 Diabetes through simulation of CD8+ T cell and beta cell interactions at tissue scale, which is difficult to observe in clinical studies.
extractions.method: Agent-based model on high-performance computing simulating a significant portion of mouse pancreas islets, incorporating stochastic T cell motility, cell cycle, differentiation, and target killing with 100 repeated simulations per scenario.
extractions.finding: Basement membrane strength delays autoimmune onset; paradoxically, beta cell regeneration increases inflammation severity through an epitope-spreading-like mechanism where proliferating beta cells increase T cell contact probability.
extractions.dataset: Simulated mouse pancreas islets with literature-derived parameters (islet diameter 100-160 μm, islet density 0.7-7.7%)
extractions.metric: Beta cell count, T cell population dynamics over 10-week simulation period
extractions.baseline: Scenarios without beta cell regeneration (0% rate)
extractions.result: Beta cell regeneration >5%/day unrealistic; basement membrane strength range 1440-20160 arbitrary units tested across 100 stochastic replicates

extractions.problem: Limited data exists on multi-serovar cross-protection of whole-cell vaccines against Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae, a major bacterial respiratory pathogen in pigs.
extractions.method: Standardized challenge dose trials (13 studies) using intranasal or aerosol chamber inoculation across 8 serovars with uniform lung lesion scoring per European Pharmacopoeia protocols; mixed-effects meta-analysis with ICC for reproducibility.
extractions.finding: The whole-cell vaccine (Coglapix) provides highly significant serovar-independent protection against both homologous (serovars 1, 2) and heterologous (serovars 4, 5, 6, 7, 9/11, 13) strains, with high reproducibility between studies.
extractions.dataset: 13 challenge trials across 8 A. pleuropneumoniae serovars, multiple pig breeds (Danbred, Hungaro-Seghers, Topigs-Norsvin), N=182 vaccinated + 161 positive controls
extractions.metric: Lung Lesion Score (LLS), p-value, intraclass correlation coefficient (ICC)
extractions.result: Homologous: p=0.00007 (serovar 1), p=0.00124 (serovar 2); heterologous: p=2.9×10⁻¹⁰ to p=0.00953; ICC=2.2-4.8% (high reproducibility except serovar 6 at 35%)

extractions.problem: Whole-cell models are the ultimate goal of computational systems biology, but the field faces challenges in data collection, model integration, and simulation speed.
extractions.method: Review of whole-cell modeling progress covering hybrid modeling methods, multi-scale simulation approaches, and high-performance computing platforms for WCM execution.
extractions.finding: WCMs have identified novel biological phenomena but their most important contribution is highlighting the discrepancy between available data and what is needed for parameterization/validation; current simulators are too slow for multicellular models.

extractions.problem: Single-host monocultures have limited protein expression capacity for consolidated bioprocessing of complex substrates; it is unclear when division of labour in consortia would be beneficial.
extractions.method: Resource-aware whole-cell ODE model adapted from the Weiße framework, explicitly modeling ribosome allocation and translational burden in a two-strain consortium vs monoculture for starch degradation.
extractions.finding: Once a burden threshold is reached, the consortium consistently outperforms the monoculture; DOL reduces ribosome redirection toward heterologous genes, enabling higher per-cell enzyme production and independent optimization of each strain.
extractions.dataset: Simulated two-strain starch degradation consortium (endohydrolase + exohydrolase), Weiße model parameters
extractions.baseline: Equivalent single-cell monoculture co-expressing both enzymes
extractions.result: Consortium outperforms monoculture above burden threshold; each consortium cell produces more of its enzyme per cell than the monoculture

extractions.problem: Using carriage prevalence as a vaccine trial endpoint is complicated by nonlinear prevalence-incidence relationships, multiple pathogen strains, and age-dependent natural immunity, making sample size estimation difficult.
extractions.method: Individual-based computational model of pneumococcal carriage incorporating serotype-specific and non-specific immunity, fitted to epidemiological settings, with simulated vaccine trials at different ages and transmission intensities.
extractions.finding: Sampling time and age at vaccination strongly influence required sample size; infant trials in lower-transmission settings with optimal sampling windows (15-24 months post-vaccination) dramatically reduce participant requirements.
extractions.dataset: Simulated pneumococcal carriage in two transmission settings (under-five prevalence: 66% higher, 55% lower), 50 simulation runs per scenario
extractions.metric: Required sample size, carriage prevalence reduction
extractions.result: Vaccine efficacy of 3/5/10 colonization equivalents (26%/39%/63% duration reduction) reduces prevalence up to 7%/10%/17%; infant trial in lower-transmission setting needs <800/330/110 participants

extractions.problem: The COVID-19 transition to remote learning required adapting an in-person cell culture lab practical to a virtual assessment format.
extractions.method: Virtual lab practical administered via course LMS using video tutorials, data analysis questions, multiple-choice questions, and a hemocytometer simulator.
extractions.finding: Average student scores and grade distributions were comparable between in-person and virtual formats, with fewer grading concerns and reduced student stress in the virtual version.
extractions.dataset: Introductory BME lab course students (in-person vs virtual cohorts)
extractions.result: Comparable scores and grade distributions across formats; fewer grading concerns for virtual practical

extractions.problem: Extracting, integrating, and presenting 3D representations of cells that are both scientifically accurate and visually appealing poses substantial computational and aesthetic challenges.
extractions.method: Collaboration of computer artists and cell biologists using Maya, Zbrush, Houdini, and Unity3D game engine; protein structures from PDB, receptor densities from flow cytometry, lipid bilayer from CHARMM-GUI simulation.
extractions.finding: Produced Nanoscape, a multi-scale explorable 3D cell environment; receptor density simulations based on MDA-MB-231 flow cytometry data; immersive virtual field trips may improve understanding of cellular complexity.
extractions.dataset: MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cell surface receptor data (CD44, EGFR, EpCAM, Her2, ICAM1, αVβ3 integrin), PDB protein structures, CHARMM-GUI lipid simulations

extractions.problem: Computational tools for in silico analysis of how cell growth and division affect photosynthesis are scarce, limiting understanding of leaf anatomy–physiology relationships.
extractions.method: Virtual leaf tissue generator based on Voronoi tessellation and cell growth model coupled to a 2D microscale CO2 gas transport model; 270 mesophyll geometries generated with varying growth anisotropy, growth extent, and airspace formation.
extractions.finding: Virtual tissue was statistically similar to real tomato leaf tissue; light-saturated photosynthesis is influenced by interactive effects of growth anisotropy, growth extent, and airspace formation through exposed mesophyll surface area.
extractions.dataset: Tomato (Solanum lycopersicum L.) leaf tissue, 270 virtual mesophyll geometries, 5 real leaf cross-section replicates
extractions.metric: KS test p-value, mean cell area, aspect ratio, Lm (mesophyll surface area per leaf area), net photosynthesis rate (A)
extractions.baseline: Real tomato leaf tissue microscopy cross-sections
extractions.result: KS test p-values: 41% (spongy cell area), 70% (spongy aspect ratio), 37% (palisade cell area), 18% (palisade aspect ratio) — distributions not significantly different

extractions.problem: The Warburg effect (aerobic glycolysis) is energetically counter-intuitive; the role of metabolic symbiosis between oxygenated and hypoxic tumour cells in promoting tumour growth is not well understood.
extractions.method: Multi-scale agent-based model in NetLogo integrating diffusion-reaction equations, stoichiometric equations, and Boolean gene regulatory networks (MAPK pathway); sensitivity analysis via Latin hypercube sampling and partial rank correlation.
extractions.finding: Metabolic symbiosis reduces glucose depletion and promotes glycolysis by depleting oxygen at the tumour boundary; blocking glucose transporters (GLUT1) is predicted to be more effective at reducing tumour growth than blocking lactate intake transporters (MCT1).
extractions.dataset: Breast cancer cell lines RNAseq data (normoxic/hypoxic), simulated tumour organoid
extractions.metric: Symbiosis index, OXPHOS/glycolytic population ratio, partial rank correlation coefficient
extractions.result: OXPHOS population ~4.5% of glycolytic under MCT1 knockout vs co-existing populations under symbiosis; minimum glucose level for glycolysis identified as key sensitivity parameter
